title: CSS fix verified via MCP browser appears not applied — stylesheet served from cache
summary: chrome-devtools MCP navigate_page type:url does not bypass subresource cache even with ignoreCache:true; edited CSS/JS keeps loading from cache and the agent misdiagnoses its own fix as broken. Only type:reload honors ignoreCache.
mistake: After editing a stylesheet, the agent re-verified with navigate_page type:url + ignoreCache:true and found the change absent from computed styles. The document was re-fetched but CSS came from the HTTP cache (the local dev server sent Last-Modified, and Chrome's heuristic caching kept the old body). The agent nearly went hunting for a CSS bug that did not exist — document.styleSheets showed the old rule while fetch(url, {cache:'no-store'}) returned the new file.
instead: After changing CSS/JS, re-verify with navigate_page type:reload + ignoreCache:true on the already-open page (this is the hard-reload path that flushes subresources). When a fix 'did not apply', settle it in one evaluate_script: compare the live rule (for (const s of document.styleSheets) for (const r of s.cssRules) ...) against fetch(cssUrl, {cache:'no-store'}).then(r=>r.text()) — if they differ, it is caching, not your CSS. Serving dev files with Cache-Control: no-store avoids the class entirely.
